title: Week 16 queue mode architecture diagram
mermaid:
flowchart LR
    U["Users and editor"] --> MAIN["main n8n process: UI, API, triggers"]
    WH["Webhook providers"] --> LB["load balancer"]
    LB --> WPH["webhook processor pool"]
    LB --> MAIN
    MAIN --> REDIS["Redis: queue / message broker"]
    WPH --> REDIS
    REDIS --> W1["worker 1: n8n worker --concurrency=5"]
    REDIS --> W2["worker 2: n8n worker --concurrency=5"]
    W1 --> PG["PostgreSQL: workflows, credentials, executions"]
    W2 --> PG
    MAIN --> PG
    WPH --> PG
    W1 --> OBJ["S3/database binary data storage"]
    W2 --> OBJ
    MAIN --> LOG["centralized logs and metrics"]
    WPH --> LOG
    W1 --> LOG
    W2 --> LOG

sequence.action: Creates an execution record in PostgreSQL and enqueues the execution ID in Redis.
Claims an execution ID from Redis.
Executes the workflow and writes results to PostgreSQL.
Posts completion to Redis so the main process can observe completion.

components.role: Serves UI and API, handles triggers, creates executions, performs at-most-once tasks, and coordinates queue mode.
Executes production workflows from Redis jobs and writes results back to PostgreSQL.
Optional ingress scaling layer for high-volume webhook requests.
requiredConfiguration: load balancer route /webhook/* to webhook processors
load balancer route /webhook-waiting/* to webhook processors
N8N_DISABLE_PRODUCTION_MAIN_PROCESS=true when disabling production webhooks on main

components.role: Durable database for workflows, credentials, users, executions, and settings.
Stores binary data in a queue-mode compatible location.
requiredConfiguration: filesystem mode is not used with queue mode
S3 lifecycle if external storage is used

components.role: Collects logs and metrics from main, webhook processors, workers, Redis, PostgreSQL, and load balancer.
requiredConfiguration: alerts for queue waiting, failed jobs, worker restarts, DB connections, and webhook 5xx

routingRules.path: editor, static files, internal API, settings

mustNotDo: Do not add the main process to the webhook load balancer pool when webhook processors exist.
Do not use filesystem binary data storage with queue mode.
Do not run workers with a different N8N_ENCRYPTION_KEY than main.
Do not scale worker count beyond PostgreSQL connection capacity.
Do not start with Kubernetes before Redis queue mode and worker operations are understood.
